name: Airport / Central Airport / Cultural Center
capture_notes: Ground-truth anchor: Super EV Hub about 130 sessions/day typical and about 300 peak high season; Central Airport 12 guns could capture about 140-170/day base if access and visibility are strong

name: Fa Ham / Central Festival / Big C Extra / Green Park
capture_notes: Strong retail and Route 118/Superhighway heat, but dense competition from Tesla, EA, Green Park/Fair Super Charge, Meta Mall, and nearby chargers

name: Nimman / Maya / One Nimman / CMU / Suan Dok
capture_notes: High lifestyle, tourist, student, condo, and hospital heat; DC hub capture depends heavily on parking control and access

name: Hang Dong / Kad Farang / Route 108 / Mae Hia
capture_notes: Affluent residential and tourism corridor; attractive if the site is true roadside with food, restroom, and easy ingress/egress

name: Mae Rim / Chotana / Route 107
capture_notes: Tourism corridor and north-side residential demand; stronger on weekend and holiday peaks than ordinary weekdays

name: San Sai / Mae Jo / Route 118
capture_notes: Residential growth and Route 118 traffic; capture is sensitive because existing chargers already shape the catchment

name: San Kamphaeng / Bo Sang / Mae On
capture_notes: Corridor heat exists, but Punn Suk shows that site readiness can materially reduce capture; use conservative assumptions unless frontage and amenities are strong

name: Old City / Tha Phae / Night Bazaar
capture_notes: Tourist and hotel density is high, but DC fast charging is constrained by parking, streets, and access friction
